assumptions: Solar is treated as low-cost non-dispatchable energy; availability is separate.
Wind is treated as low-cost non-dispatchable energy; availability is separate.

source: Public nuclear import context plus synthetic merit order
assumptions: Nuclear/import-equivalent supply is lower variable cost than fossil generation.
Equivalent supply is ordered between low-cost imports and local thermal defaults.
Gas is modeled ahead of coal on emissions and flexibility grounds.
Equivalent supply is a modeling device for sparse topology and not a real unit.
Coal is modeled after gas because of higher emissions and slower flexibility.
Waste-to-energy is dispatchable but not treated as primary low-cost supply.
Unknown generators are deliberately placed late in the merit order.
Fallback equivalent capacity is used only to keep sparse islands solvable.
